mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a direct, high-frequency indicator of broad economic health, signaling robust corporate revenue and earnings growth which drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate near the zero lower bound, a significant month-over-month increase signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle. This increases the discount rate for future earnings and reduces risk appetite, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.10pp month-over-month while prior level was < 0.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rapidly accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates an overheating economy. This interaction forces the market to price in aggressive future monetary tightening, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 2% AND 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL > 3%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative mean forward return (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods compared to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, a consistent decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms improving labor market health and boosts consumer and investor confidence, leading to positive equity returns as recovery expectations solidify.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (predicting positive returns) is below 55% during periods where year-over-year GDPC1 growth is positive but below its 10-year average.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing and supply-side activity. This is a leading indicator of corporate revenues and earnings strength, supporting higher equity prices over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a rank correlation below 0.03 with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as housing is a major, interest-rate-sensitive component of GDP. This decline signals lower future growth, negatively impacting the broader equity market.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 15% from its 6-month high
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger, the S&P 500 produces positive returns more than 50% of the time over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains anchored, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This interaction creates policy uncertainty and fears of unanchored inflation expectations, which is negative for risk assets.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4% AND FEDFUNDS is unchanged over 3 months
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on trigger events fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle (peak-to-peak).

mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year growth rate of real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic health. A positive and stable growth rate underpins aggregate corporate earnings growth and justifies higher equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between year-over-year GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 10-year rolling period.
